flashcards.front: What is an enumeration (enum) in Java?
flashcards.back: An enum is a special data type that defines a fixed set of constants. It provides type-safe way to represent a group of related values, introduced in Java 5.

flashcards.front: When were enumerations introduced in Java?
How were constants defined before Java 5?
flashcards.back: Before Java 5, programmers used int constants (public static final int) or String constants which lacked type safety and were prone to errors.

flashcards.front: What is type safety in the context of enums?
flashcards.back: Type safety ensures that enum variables can only hold values defined in the enum. The compiler prevents assigning invalid values, unlike int constants.

flashcards.front: Can enums have constructors in Java?
flashcards.back: Yes, enums can have constructors. The constructor is invoked once for each enum constant and can be private (or package-private).

flashcards.front: Can enums have methods in Java?
flashcards.back: Yes, enums can have both instance methods and static methods. They can also override methods from interfaces or parent class.

flashcards.front: Can enums implement interfaces in Java?
flashcards.back: Yes, enums can implement any number of interfaces. This allows enums to define behavior through interface methods.

flashcards.front: What is the superclass of all enums in Java?
flashcards.back: All enums implicitly extend java.lang.Enum class. This class provides methods like values(), valueOf(), name(), ordinal(), and compareTo().

flashcards.front: What is the syntax to define a basic enum in Java?
flashcards.back: Use the 'enum' keyword: enum EnumName { CONSTANT1, CONSTANT2, CONSTANT3 }

flashcards.front: What is the values() method in enums?
flashcards.back: The values() method is automatically generated for all enums. It returns an array containing all enum constants in their declaration order.

flashcards.front: What is the valueOf() method in enums?
flashcards.back: The valueOf(String name) method returns the enum constant with the specified name. It throws IllegalArgumentException if the name doesn't match any constant.
Yes, enum constants can have arguments passed to the constructor. Syntax: CONSTANT(arg1, arg2)
Yes, enums can declare abstract methods, and each enum constant must provide its own implementation of these abstract methods.

flashcards.front: How do you use enums in switch statements?
flashcards.back: Enums can be used directly in switch statements without the enum name prefix. Example: switch(day) { case MONDAY: ... }

flashcards.front: What is the ordinal() method in enums?
flashcards.back: The ordinal() method returns the position of the enum constant in its enum declaration, starting from zero (similar to array index).
